text: Former Bayern Munich legend Franz Beckenbauer has suggested that Jurgen Klopp could replace Pep Guardiola at Bayern Munich, should the latter leave in the future. Klopp, who won two Bundesliga titles with Borussia Dortmund, has announced his departure from the club in the summer. Beckenbauer, who managed Bayern twice, believes Klopp would be a good fit for Bayern. Meanwhile, in the Champions League, Bayern have been drawn against Barcelona, and Beckenbauer believes only a fully fit Bayern team can beat Lionel Messi and Barcelona. He highlights the importance of players like Franck Ribery, David Alaba, and Arjen Robben in Bayern's quest for success against Barcelona.

Esteban Cambiasso, a former Argentina international and current Leicester City midfielder, has stated that helping Leicester City avoid relegation would be a career highlight equivalent to winning a trophy. Cambiasso, who previously won five Serie A titles and the Champions League at Inter Milan, signed with Leicester last summer on a free transfer. He acknowledges the challenge of staying in the Premier League with Leicester, who are currently bottom of the table, but believes the team has a chance to win matches and avoid relegation. Cambiasso will be hoping to lead Leicester to a crucial victory against West Ham United on Saturday.

Jonathan Trott, who last played a Test in 2013 due to stress-related illness, had a short-lived return to Test cricket as he was dismissed for a duck in the first over against the West Indies. This time, Trott, traditionally a middle order batsman, was captained by Alastair Cook as an opening partner. His promotion to the top order, a strategic move to replace Adam Lyth who is yet to debut for England, proved unsuccessful. The wicket left England at 1-1 after just five balls in the first Test in Antigua. Trott's return was his first Test appearance since his Ashes turmoil against Mitchell Johnson 18 months ago.

Twelve-year-old Shannon Hayes from Carmarthenshire, Wales believes her pet hen has laid the smallest chicken egg in the world, with a length of 1.8cm, potentially breaking the previous record by two millimeters. The tiny egg, smaller than a 5p coin, was found among ordinary-sized eggs in her hen coop. Shannon is keeping the potential record-breaking egg in the fridge for safe-keeping, as the previous records were set in 2011 and 2014 by eggs from the United States and Somerset, respectively.

Leeds United, already grappling with managerial instability, suffered a setback on Friday as six players claimed injuries and withdrew from the game against Charlton. These players include four Italians, a Frenchman, and an Albanian, most of whom were signed by banned president Massimo Cellino. The team's physio and head coach Neil Redfearn were reportedly unaware of the injuries. Several players, including Mirco Antenucci, Giuseppe Bellusci, and Marco Silvestri, took to Twitter to clarify their injuries, with Antenucci and Silvestri stating they sustained their injuries in training and a match respectively. The ongoing absence of these key players, coupled with the uncertain future of Redfearn's contract

The article discusses a significant change in the fashion style of SNP leader Nicola Sturgeon. She has moved away from her previous boxy jackets and severe suits, opting for a more glamorous and svelte appearance. In particular, she was seen in a fuchsia column dress on the BBC's Andrew Marr Show, which flattered her physique. This new style is believed to be the result of hiring a personal shopper and stylist before the 2007 Scottish parliament election. Sturgeon's new look includes skirt suits that cinch at the waist, lighter and shorter hair, and a preference for Kurt Geiger shoes. Her previous appearances, such as modeling clothes made from blankets in a charity event in 2

A hardware store worker, John R. Lind, has been found guilty of ejaculating in his female colleague's coffee as a means to gain attention. Pat Maahs, the victim, initially believed the coffee smelled bad due to spoiled milk. Lind, who pleaded guilty to indecent exposure, will be sentenced for the misdemeanor on May 22. The incident prompted Maahs to propose legislation that would make such actions a felony, stating, "I felt that there was a hole in the law, and it needed to be plugged."

Former Liverpool defender Jamie Carragher has named Chelsea captain John Terry as the best defender to have played in the Premier League, citing Terry's impressive performance in Chelsea's 0-0 draw against Arsenal on Sunday. Carragher believes Terry has been instrumental in Chelsea's title charge this season, with his defensive display against Arsenal being particularly noteworthy. Chelsea manager Jose Mourinho also praised Terry, claiming that the defender's performance against Arsenal was the best he has ever seen from him. The draw at the Emirates means Chelsea now need just six points from five games to claim their first Premier League title since 2010.

The police are conducting a search in the patio of the Royal Standard pub in Ely, Cambridgeshire, in connection with the 17-year-old murder investigation of its former landlady, Deborah Steel. Deborah, 37, disappeared in 1997, and her body has never been found. The investigation has recently been reclassified as a murder inquiry, and a 73-year-old man is currently on bail, while the bail of two other individuals has been cancelled. The search has the full consent of the current landlord, and so far, no clues have been found. The family of Deborah, particularly her sister Gini Secker, 47, are hoping for answers to

In the highly anticipated $300m boxing match between Manny Pacquiao and Floyd Mayweather on May 2, Pacquiao has vowed to approach the fight with a warrior's mentality, promising to give his all in each round. He has been installed as the underdog for the bout, which is being billed as the biggest sporting event in history. Pacquiao, who has been training in Los Angeles, expressed confidence in his ability to win, stating that he knows what it takes to defeat Mayweather and will leave no doubt about the outcome. He also mentioned that if the opportunity to knock out Mayweather arises, he will take it. Meanwhile, Mayweather has been using a Cryosauna for training.

The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) is searching for the individual who graffiti-tagged a police horse named Charly in Venice Beach. The tagger, who managed to spray the horse with 'RBS' and an arrow during daylight hours, is believed to be a local vandal who usually tags stationary objects and facades. Authorities are hoping to identify the person by deciphering the tag and are asking for public help. The paint was removed from Charly without issue, but the LAPD is still seeking information about the incident, asking witnesses to contact 310-482-6313 or Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (800-22

A 13-year-old boy from Medina, Ohio allegedly stole $25,000 from his grandfather and distributed $100 notes among his school friends at Claggett Middle School. The money was used by both children and adults for a shopping spree, with approximately $7,000 recovered as of Saturday. Police are still investigating the case, planning to file charges once they have identified all parties involved. The school principal, T.J. Hellickson, has confirmed cooperation with law enforcement.

The president of Bastia, Pierre-Marie Geronimi, has demanded the resignation of French League (LFP) president Frederic Thiriez following his absence from the pitch during the League Cup final. Geronimi expressed his dissatisfaction as Thiriez did not shake hands with the players before the match, which Bastia lost 4-0 to Paris Saint Germain. The incident is particularly significant for Geronimi, as Thiriez failed to present Bastia with their Ligue 2 champions' trophy in 2012. This latest incident has further strained the relationship between the two parties.

The article describes a prank gone wrong between two Scottish teenagers, Hayleigh Mcbay and David Clarke. Hayleigh, on April Fool's Day, pretended to dump David via a WhatsApp message. However, David retaliated by also pretending to break up with her. Shocked, Hayleigh revealed the prank on Twitter, but received backlash from trolls who found it staged. Hayleigh clarified that the breakup was not planned and that David had guessed it was an April Fool's Day joke. Despite the negative comments, Hayleigh stated that she would not prank her mother about being pregnant the following day. The article includes a screenshot of the WhatsApp conversation between Hayle

U.S. Senator Kelly Ayotte's senior aide, David Wihby, has resigned after his arrest for soliciting a prostitute in Nashua, New Hampshire. Wihby, who was Ayotte's state director and her longtime friend, has also served as a former deputy commissioner and interim commissioner of the state Department of Labor, and currently holds an elected seat on the Manchester school board. Ayotte expressed shock and sadness over the incident and stated that Wihby could not continue his duties, accepting his resignation. Ten men were arrested in Nashua over the last week on prostitution charges, with Wihby being one of them. He was released on $2,000 personal recognizance and will be arra

Arsene Wenger, the Arsenal manager, deliberates over his starting line-up for matches throughout the week, often deciding on the morning of the game. He revealed in an interview that he maintains a preliminary list of 8-9 players, with a lingering doubt about one or two. Wenger's selection process is a mix of scientific analysis and intuition, considering the balance of the team, performance in training, and other factors such as experience and confidence under pressure. Despite making the final decision on game day, Wenger has his team in mind for the entire week, often contemplating the line-up and dealing with uncertainties throughout the week. Wenger's strategies have led to Arsenal's success, securing three Premier League

In the Friday night episode of Jeopardy, contestant Tom, a freemason, provided an inappropriate answer when asked about the age of puberty. Instead of the expected response, Tom answered "What is the age of consent?" causing viewers at home and potential awkwardness in the studio. The incorrect answer sparked outrage on Twitter, with many viewers expressing shock and concern about Tom's apparent misunderstanding.

Tennis star Rafael Nadal began his clay court season with a confident 6-2, 6-1 victory over Lucas Pouille in the Monte Carlo Masters. Nadal, ranked No 5, played a measured game with only five unforced errors and didn't fully utilize his forehand power. Pouille, a talented 21-year-old, displayed some attacking flair but was unable to consistently challenge Nadal. This win marks the start of Nadal's attempt to rebuild his self-belief following recent quarter-final losses in Indian Wells and Miami. Despite not winning Monte Carlo since 2012, Nadal will next face either John Isner or Viktor Troicki in the third round.

A group of individuals held an annual fishing event on the Auckland coast, where they competed with homemade boats made from household items such as a couch strapped to tires and a floating Subaru car. The event caused a unique call-out for the Auckland coastguards, who assisted in the rescue of participants, one of whom capsized outside the harbor. Despite the capsize, no one was injured. The event, which has been happening for years, is aimed at spreading positivity within the community and is known for participants building their own boats. The police issued a warning to the participants but no charges were laid.

A British trader, Navinder Sarao, is accused of contributing to a £500 billion Wall Street crash in 2010 from his home in Hounslow, West London. Sarao, who was arrested last week, is alleged to have made the 'Flash Crash' through his trading activities. In an email from last October, he boasted that he could earn £1 million in a week during volatile market conditions. The email, submitted as evidence, also revealed his request for an urgent £4.25 million from a company he set up in a Caribbean tax haven. Sarao has remained in custody since his court appearance last week due to a delay in providing the £5 million bail.

The official advertisement for the highly anticipated boxing match between Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao has been released, with less than a month until the event in Las Vegas. The 30-second clip, filmed in a Los Angeles studio, shows both fighters squaring up in the middle of a desert road, with the MGM Grand and 'Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas' sign visible in the background. The fight is expected to break the pay-per-view record set by Mayweather's fight with Saul 'Canelo' Alvarez, with Sky winning the bidding war to screen the event in the UK, charging customers £19.95 for access. The fight takes place at the MGM Grand in less than

Rory McIlroy, the World No 1 golfer, participated in a match of Circular Soccer, a new football game designed to enhance skill and ball control, defeating Jamie Dornan, best known for his role in Fifty Shades of Grey. McIlroy, who recently placed fourth in the Masters tournament, is an avid Manchester United fan and revealed his childhood dream of playing for the team. Circular Soccer, devised by Jamie Dornan's father and other experts, has been integrated into FIFA's Global Grassroots Youth Programmes and has been tested by Manchester United's elite academy, suggesting its potential popularity.

A 73-year-old woman, Colleen Ann Harris, also known as 'Grandma Cokie', has been found guilty of murdering her third husband, Robert Harris, in 2013. The verdict was delivered by a California jury after a two-hour trial. Harris, who wept in court upon hearing the verdict, could face a sentence of 50 years to life. This conviction comes three decades after she was acquitted of killing her second husband in 1985, a case in which she claimed self-defense due to ongoing abuse.

In preparation for their highly anticipated fight, boxing legends Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao spent quality time with their families over the weekend. Pacquiao was accompanied by his wife Jinkee and their five children in Los Angeles, while Mayweather spent time with his girlfriend Doralie Medina. Pacquiao and his family recently purchased a mansion in Beverly Hills, and the couple is widely celebrated in the Philippines. Mayweather, who is dating Medina since last year, has been visiting the SubZero Recovery in Las Vegas for recovery sessions, while Pacquiao has been training in Los Angeles. Both fighters are expected to weigh in at no more than 154lbs this coming weekend, marking the beginning of their final week of

Martin Guptill, known for his impressive performance in the recent World Cup, has been recalled to the New Zealand Test squad after a long absence, for their upcoming series against England. Guptill's teammate, Matt Henry, a 23-year-old paceman, is also called up for his first Test, having played only 20 first-class matches. Coach Mike Hesson expressed optimism about both players, particularly Guptill, stating he can be as influential in the longer format as he is in the shorter formats. Notably, the squad will play without Daniel Vettori, who retired after the seven-wicket loss to Australia, and James Neesham due to a hamstring injury. Mitchell Santner is the only uncapped player

In the 2015 NCAA Men's Basketball Final Four, University of Kentucky guard Andrew Harrison directed a racial slur at University of Wisconsin player Frank Kaminsky during a post-game press conference, which was broadcast live. Harrison later apologized for his comments, stating they were intended as a joke and were not meant to be disrespectful. Kaminsky, in response, accepted Harrison's apology and wished him luck in future endeavors. The incident occurred after Kentucky's loss to Wisconsin, ending their undefeated season with a record of 38-1. Harrison finished the game with 13 points and four assists, and his twin brother, Aaron, was also projected as an NBA draft pick. The Wildcats will now face

Scottish comedian Limmy has shocked his fans by posting a video showing a large spider crawling out of his mouth and across his face. In the seven-second clip, Limmy maintains a manic grin while the three-inch house spider walks out of his mouth and along his cheek, which he described as a self-imposed 'bushtucker trial.' The spider seems unharmed by the experience, causing some viewers to express revulsion, while others praised the video as the best thing they've ever seen. One viewer even wondered how the spider knew not to go down Limmy's throat, and another jokingly asked how many spiders he had eaten.

Andras Janos Vass, 25, a Hungarian man, has been convicted of human trafficking and racketeering in Miami-Dade, Florida. Vass lured young, gay Eastern European men to the United States, promising them easy work and high wages, only to take their travel documents, threaten their families, and force them into a life of sex slavery. The victims were made to work 20 hours a day on sex work, in person and on webcams. At least two victims were lured to America via the Planet Romeo gay dating website, and Vass is accused of running the sex ring with two other men, Gabor Acs and Viktor Berki, who have yet to stand trial. Vass faces a maximum

Beijing International Airport is building the world's largest terminal, Terminal 1, in response to a significant increase in Chinese tourists traveling globally. The terminal, opening in 2018, will span 700,000 square meters and handle 45 million passengers yearly. Designed by Zaha Hadid in collaboration with ADPI, the terminal features a six-tier concept aiming to decrease walking distances and increase connectivity. The design emphasizes open spaces, with a central courtyard serving as a global meeting place, reflecting the harmonious blend of Chinese culture and landscape. The new terminal is part of Daxing Airport, the second busiest international airport in the world, which saw 96 million passengers last year, behind H

On Thursday, Bondi's iconic Icebergs Club pool was drained to serve as an unusual catwalk setting for the Ten Pieces presentation during Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia. The monochrome unisex collection, designed by the label co-founded by Icebergs restaurateur Maurice Terzini and Lucy Hinckfuss, was showcased through models walking through the pool and break wall, offering a stunning ocean backdrop. The minimalist, androgynous designs were complemented by nibbles from Icebergs and drinks from Santa Vittoria, while guests basked in the sunny weather. The event attracted attention from beachgoers and passers-by, blending the worlds of fashion and leisure

The French Spiderman, Alain Robert, has scaled Dubai's Cayan Tower, the world's tallest twisted skyscraper, without safety equipment. Using only sticky tape and chalk, Robert, aged 52, climbed the 75-story, 1,007ft tower that twists 90 degrees in an hour and a half. The climb was captured on video and showed Robert struggling to find footing on narrow ledges. At one point, he waved a flag in homage to the Cayan Group, who had given him permission to climb the tower. Despite previous falls during climbs, Robert has a history of scaling buildings illegally, resulting in arrests, imprisonments, and bans

Manchester City manager Manuel Pellegrini is under pressure due to the team's poor performance this season, with a dwindling challenge for the title and a battle to secure a top-four finish. Despite this, former England international Stuart Pearce believes Pellegrini deserves more time to turn the team around and should remain in his position. Pearce attributes City's poor form to the fading influence of Yaya Toure, who has had a largely ineffective season, and praises Sergio Aguero as a rare shining light in the underperforming squad.

A married couple, Derek Lowe (38) and Tina Lowe (33), were fatally struck by an Amtrak train in Durham, North Carolina, on Easter Sunday morning. The accident occurred on property owned by Norfolk-Southern, and the train, heading from Charlotte to New York City, continued its journey after a delay of about 2 hours and 45 minutes. None of the 166 passengers on the train were injured, though a man who fell ill during the delay did receive treatment. Reports suggest that the Lowes might have been homeless and living in a nearby camp. A local who interacted with them expressed sympathy, urging kindness towards those in need. The accident occurred approximately 25 miles from the train

Paul Gregory, 51, from Bedford, was fined £795 for dishonestly obtaining a blue badge, claiming he could only walk 200 meters, despite being an avid walker and member of a mountaineering club. Council officials discovered his fraud when they read an article about him completing a 191-mile hike in Cumbria and a subsequent weekend walk in Wales, which he had applied for the blue badge shortly after. He was sentenced to 150 hours of unpaid work, a one-year supervision order, and 19 sessions of 'thinking skills'. This case was considered one of the most outrageous instances of blue badge fraud by Bedford Borough Council.

Manchester United are interested in signing Hugo Lloris from Tottenham Hotspur, with concerns about David de Gea's future at Old Trafford. However, Spurs are valuing Lloris at a £35million price tag to discourage potential suitors, particularly domestic rivals. Spurs chairman Daniel Levy, known for his tough negotiation skills, is determined to keep Lloris at White Hart Lane. The current world record transfer fee for a goalkeeper is £32.6million paid by Juventus for Gianluigi Buffon in 2001, but Levy values Lloris even higher than that. This high price tag and Levy's negotiating style have caused hesitancy in United

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) experienced technical difficulties during the recent tax filing season, with over 8 million calls being disconnected. Only 40% of those who managed to get through waited more than 30 minutes to speak with an agent. IRS Commissioner John Koskinen attributed these issues to budget cuts, stating that customer service has significantly deteriorated since 2010 due to a $1.2 billion reduction in the agency's budget. Koskinen pointed out that these cuts were necessary to fund the implementation of President Barack Obama's health law, as the IRS is required by law to administer it.

Jeremy Clarkson, former Top Gear presenter, made a humorous remark at a charity auction in the Cotswolds, joking about swearing freely now that he's no longer with the BBC. He also joked about trawling the job center for hosting jobs. Clarkson, who is known for his unconventional humor, mistook a male bidder for a female and humorously commented on his previous work with James May. The event was Clarkson's first public appearance since his sacking from the BBC for an alleged assault incident in 2015. He was initially scheduled to make a TV comeback as a guest host on BBC1's Have I Got News for You, but he withdrew from the show two weeks before film

Chilean pickpockets are reportedly the most effective thieves operating in central London, according to undercover police. These gangs, often consisting of up to six members, follow wealthy shoppers in the West End and steal cash, belongings, and even luxury items. One example involves two Chilean women wearing burkas who were stopped by police after being spotted targeting rich Arab tourists in the Park Lane and Mayfair areas, with a bag containing €130,000, £20,000, and gold and diamond jewellery. London's busy shopping areas attract pickpockets from various countries, including Romania, Algeria, and Bulgaria, but DC Darren Bond, in charge of monitoring Oxford Street, Bond

In a tragic incident at the Arcadia Palms apartment complex in Las Vegas, two children have died following an accidental vehicle fire. Four children were playing near a parked minivan when one of them unintentionally started a fire. Two children managed to escape, while the other two became trapped and could not be rescued. Police are investigating the incident but do not suspect any criminal activity due to the child's age. The names and causes of the fire are yet to be released. Witnesses reported hearing a loud explosion and a woman shouting for her child and nephew. The victims are believed to be related, and the community is mourning the loss.

Javier Hernandez scored the decisive goal for Real Madrid in the Champions League quarter-final against Atletico Madrid, securing a 1-0 aggregate victory. The Santiago Bernabeu stadium was electric with fan support as shown in a video uploaded by defender Raphael Varane, who captured the team's entrance to the stadium. The atmosphere was intense, with fans mobbing the team coach, banging on windows, and lighting flares in anticipation. Real Madrid could potentially face Spanish rivals Barcelona, Juventus, or Bayern Munich in the semi-finals, with the draw set for Friday.

On NBC's Today show, a graphic erroneously identified Saturday Night Live creator Lorne Michaels as 'Lauren'. The mistake was corrected for subsequent broadcasts and online. The interview with Michaels, aged 70, was part of a feature on a New York gathering of TIME magazine's 100 most influential people. Michaels, a Canadian, is renowned for creating Saturday Night Live, which has garnered 160 Emmy Award nominations and 35 wins, and has launched numerous careers. He was honored by both TIME and Billboard magazines for his significant influence in comedy.

During an Easter Sunday service at the Korean Union United Methodist Church in Rahway, New Jersey, the entire roof unexpectedly collapsed, causing 14 people to be hospitalized with minor to serious injuries. The incident occurred during a service by the Manantial de Vida Pentecostal congregation, which rents the church space. The cause of the collapse is under investigation, and the building will not be re-entered until an engineering firm has assessed its stability. Remarkably, no one was killed in the incident, leading the Rahway Police Chief to attribute the outcome to divine intervention on Easter Sunday.

German Chancellor Angela Merkel and her husband, chemistry professor Joachim Sauer, spent their annual Easter holiday on the island of Ischia, Italy, despite less than ideal weather conditions. The couple traditionally stay at the five-star Miramare Spa Hotel, known for its private beach, thermal gardens spa, and luxurious accommodations. Due to the cooler weather, Merkel was reported to have avoided the pool and lounges, instead opting for Pilates classes and healthy vegetarian meals. Her husband, who is a professor of theoretical chemistry, is often private and avoids the media spotlight.

Radamel Falcao, Manchester United's striker, has identified Sergio Aguero as the main threat for Manchester City in this weekend's derby. Aguero has a history of scoring against United since joining City in 2011. Falcao expressed his eagerness to play in the derby, acknowledging its significance to both sets of fans. Despite a bit-part role under Louis van Gaal, Falcao remains content at United, expressing a willingness to adapt and learn. He and Aguero previously came together at a Puma event.

Andy Murray, who recently got married, attended two Barcelona football matches within a week, watching them defeat PSG and Valencia, respectively. The second game, a Champions League quarter-final, was on April 13, with Neymar scoring a brace to secure a 2-0 victory. Murray was accompanied by his best man, Ross Hutchins, a retired tennis player. During his time in Barcelona, Murray is training on clay courts with Jonas Bjorkman and is scheduled to play in the BMW Open in Munich later this month before returning to Spain for the Madrid Masters in May.

Tennis coach Martina Navratilova, a 18-time Grand Slam singles champion, has ended her coaching partnership with Agnieszka Radwanska, the 2012 Wimbledon runner-up currently ranked No. 9. The duo worked together since announcing their collaboration in December. However, due to Navratilova's inability to fully commit to the project, they mutually agreed that it would not be feasible for a long-term partnership. Radwanska has had a challenging season, with a 13-11 record, including a first-round loss in the Stuttgart tournament this week. On April 23, St George's Day, several English football players expressed their patriotism through social media posts. Harry Kane, currently with Tottenham and England, shared a photo of himself and his brother with their faces painted with the St George's cross. Rio Ferdinand, former England captain, posted a goal celebration photo from 2003 and a picture of himself wearing an England jersey. Gary Cahill, England's current center-back, released a video with England Rugby players discussing national pride. Other players like Nathaniel Clyne, Charlie Austin, and Peter Crouch also posted celebratory messages on Twitter for St George's Day.

Cristiano Ronaldo scored his 300th goal for Real Madrid in a match against Rayo Vallecano on April 8, becoming the third highest scorer in the club's history. To celebrate this achievement, club president Florentino Perez presented Ronaldo with a special commemorative shirt with "300" on the back. Ronaldo's team-mates, including Gareth Bale, embraced him after he scored the landmark goal. However, the team will be missing French striker Karim Benzema who picked up a knee injury in the Champions League quarter-final against Atletico Madrid, and will be out for the Malaga game. Real Madrid assistant coach Fernando Hierro, a club legend who won the Champions League three times as a player, believes the current team has the potential to win the Champions League for a second consecutive season. He cites the team's strength, confidence, and recent success, particularly in last year's close final against Atletico Madrid, as reasons for his optimism. Hierro draws a parallel between the current squad and the successful Real Madrid teams of the late 1990s and early 2000s, stating that the urge to win the Champions League is ingrained in the club's DNA.

Chelsea are favored to sign Brazilian talent Kenedy, as his economic rights have been sold to agent Giuliano Bertolucci, who represents Chelsea players Oscar, Willian, and Ramires. Additionally, Kenedy is represented by former Portugal international Deco, who previously played at Stamford Bridge. While other top European clubs like Manchester United, Barcelona, and Juventus have shown interest, Chelsea appears to be in the lead to finalize a deal with Fluminense. Kenedy, a 19-year-old forward, has represented Brazil at the Under-17 and Under-20 levels and is highly regarded by the national team.

In the first leg of the Champions League quarter-final, Atletico Madrid striker Mario Mandzukic sustained injuries from physical altercations with Real Madrid players Sergio Ramos and Dani Carvajal, but is expected to play in the return match at the Bernabeu on Wednesday. Mandzukic had missed Atletico's 2-1 La Liga win over Deportivo La Coruna due to an ankle injury, but he has since returned to training. Manager Diego Simeone expects Mandzukic to be fit and has indicated that he will likely start alongside Antoine Griezmann, who is in a rich vein of form with 20 goals in La Liga. Griezmann scored both goals in Atlet

Italian authorities conducted a large-scale anti-terrorism operation on Friday, focusing on a suspected al-Qaeda cell with potential ties to Osama bin Laden. This group was reportedly discussed as planning attacks on the Vatican, as well as in Afghanistan, Pakistan, and Italy. Some of the suspects have been under surveillance since 2005, and police have found evidence of their involvement in a 2009 market bombing in Pakistan that resulted in over 300 casualties. The operation, which is ongoing, aims to arrest 18 individuals, primarily from Pakistan, and has led to the discovery of substantial weapons and cash movements. Additionally, some suspects are alleged to be involved in migrant trafficking, attempting to recruit

Real Madrid are keen on signing Manchester United's goalkeeper David De Gea, but are willing to wait until next summer if negotiations with United manager Louis van Gaal are unsuccessful. De Gea's contract expires in 2016, and he is yet to sign a new deal. Real Madrid may consider Chelsea's Petr Cech as a temporary solution if they cannot secure De Gea this summer. Meanwhile, Lionel Messi is recovering from an injury ahead of Barcelona's game against Celta Vigo, and James Rodriguez is set to return from injury for Real Madrid's game on Sunday. In Italy, Paulo Dybala, who has been linked with Arsenal, could join Inter Milan managed by former Manchester City manager Roberto M

Ben Flower, a Wales forward for Wigan Warriors, returned from a six-month suspension for punching an opponent during last season's Grand Final. Despite his team's victory, he expressed he did not feel he deserved the standing ovation he received. Flower acknowledged his mistake and emphasized the need to manage his emotions better to avoid similar incidents. Coach Shaun Wane had expressed a desire for Flower to return as a more aggressive player, but Flower clarified that aggressiveness should not equate to dirty play.

A wildlife-rescue volunteer, Sandie Konopelski, was tragically killed after responding to a call about an opossum on the tracks in southwestern Illinois. She was struck by a westbound commuter train between Swansea and Belleville on Friday morning and died at the scene due to blunt-force trauma. Konopelski, a licensed wildlife rehabilitator, was well-known for her dedication to animals through her work with the Bi-State Wildlife Hotline. The organization expressed grief over her loss and encouraged donations in her honor. Authorities are unsure why Konopelski didn't hear the approaching train, and it remains unclear if the train had been called about the opossum in this instance. Train

Kevin Rebbie, a 56-year-old resident of Limerick Township, Pennsylvania, was arrested for allegedly sexually abusing a 15-year-old girl over a four-year period and filming her with a hidden camera in her bathroom. The girl discovered the camera in March, and the investigation revealed 41 videos, including 34 of victims showering. Rebbie uploaded the videos to his computer and admitted to filming the girl specifically. The relationship between Rebbie and the girl's family is unknown, and he is currently being held on a $500,000 bail, with a preliminary hearing scheduled for May 1.

A woman, Aaronessa Keaton, was charged with manslaughter in 2014 for a head-on collision that resulted in a 5-year-old child's death. The incident occurred while Keaton, who was eight months pregnant and under the influence of marijuana and Benzodiazepines, was driving and on probation for marijuana possession. Court records indicate that Keaton showed no remorse for her actions, remarking "Shit happens" after learning of the child's death. The other child in the car, as well as the adults, survived with minor injuries. Keaton was recently arrested and indicted by a grand jury for the incident.

FIFA announced that Football Association chairman Greg Dyke returned a £16,000 Parmigiani watch he received as a gift during the World Cup. Initially, Dyke refused to return the watch, intending to donate it to Breast Cancer Care for auction. However, after facing potential sanctions, Dyke complied and returned the watch. The watch will now be auctioned for a Brazilian charity, and Dyke has pledged to make a personal donation to Breast Cancer Care. The proceedings related to a possible breach of the FIFA Code of Ethics have been closed.

QPR's Nedum Onuoha is attracting interest from multiple clubs, including Hull City, Everton, West Ham United, and Stoke City, as he considers his future after QPR may go down. Australia's largest venomous snake, a four-meter long king cobra named Raja, has produced a record-breaking yield of 496 milligrams of venom at the Australian Reptile Park in New South Wales. This amount is 40 times more than that of a brown snake and enough to fill a shot glass. Veteran handler Billy Collett, who has been milking snakes for nearly a decade, successfully extracted the venom for the first time from a king cobra. Raja's venom will be distributed among research institutes across the country, and the milking also allowed Collett to safely measure and weigh the snake, which had not been assessed for four years.

A viral video showing a wave rider surfing in front of Sydney's Opera House, initially posted in 2012, resurfaced on Twitter this week, sparking widespread speculation. The article discusses a recent study conducted by scientists at the University of Southern California, which identified a gene named Nox3 in the inner ear that plays a significant role in determining an individual's susceptibility to noise-induced hearing loss. This is the first genome-wide association study on noise-induced hearing loss in mice. The findings could potentially help identify those at higher genetic risk for hearing loss, allowing them to take preventive measures before being exposed to hazardous noise. Understanding the genetic factors affecting hearing loss is crucial in reducing the risk, as preventing hearing loss is more beneficial than attempting hearing restoration. The research was published in the journal PLOS Genetics.

Susie Clark, a resident of Evening Shade, Arkansas, found a 3.69-carat white diamond at Crater of Diamonds State Park on her last day of a visit with her husband. The park, which holds the only diamond-bearing site accessible to the public in the U.S., had recently been plowed due to rain, increasing the chances of finding diamonds on the surface. The article discusses a significant increase in back and neck problems among young people, particularly those under 30. Experts attribute this rise to increasingly sedentary lifestyles, frequent use of mobile phones, and smaller gadgets that require users to lean over them for extended periods. This strain puts extra pressure on the neck and shock-absorbing discs in the vertebrae. The problem is further exacerbated by the trend of hot-desking, as chairs are not adjusted to suit individuals. The survey also revealed that modern life is largely sedentary, with many spending long hours sitting at work and in front of TV screens. Chiropractors advise regular breaks to alleviate build-up of tension in the back.

During the White House's annual Take our Daughters and Sons to Work Day, a young girl named Anya Brodie asked Michelle Obama her age. Per Mertesacker, a central defender for Arsenal, attributes the team's improved performance this season to bringing back a "arguing culture" that was lost following a series of heavy defeats last season, including a 6-3 loss to Manchester City and a 5-1 loss to Liverpool. Mertesacker believes that confrontations and open discussions are crucial for a team's mentality, as they help find solutions and maintain unity. This season, Arsenal is in a strong position, closing in on Manchester City in second place, and they will face Liverpool in a crucial match for both teams on Saturday, with the Champions League places at stake.

An Oregon teenager, Miranda Ashley Calvin, was arrested on Sunday afternoon for allegedly hitting a pedestrian, 49-year-old Jerrie Ann Hornig, with her car and fleeing the scene in Gresham on Saturday night. The incident occurred around 8.30pm, and when police arrived, they found Hornig dead. Calvin was identified and located at a Marriott Hotel in Clackamas and was arrested for manslaughter, felony hit-and-run, and reckless driving. Hornig's son, Maxwell Horning, shared a Facebook post remembering his mother and expressing his grief, stating that she was in a better place and that he missed her dearly. Martin Skrtel, Liverpool defender and Slovakia international, has dismissed rumors of a move away from the Premier League club. Despite being linked with German side Wolfsburg and Italian outfit Napoli, Skrtel has declared he will not be leaving Liverpool, even though his contract expires in 2016. His agent, Karol Csonto, has confirmed that Skrtel is close to finalizing a new contract with Liverpool, and there is currently no offer from Wolfsburg on the table. Skrtel made over 200 league appearances for Liverpool since joining in 2008, and he played in the Reds' 2-1 FA Cup semi-final defeat to Aston Villa on Sunday.

A 20-year-old woman named Britney E. Montenegro was arrested in Orlando, Florida, early Saturday after a fight involving multiple individuals spilled out onto the streets. The incident, which occurred around 2.40 am at East Pine Street and South Court Avenue, led to Montenegro being charged with disorderly conduct. Despite being covered in blood, Montenegro, who is originally from Queens, New York, and studies at Valencia Community College, was relatively unscathed. The fight is believed to have started inside a bar and Montenegro allegedly attempted to attack another woman.

Lucas Moura, a Paris Saint-Germain player, has expressed his admiration for fellow South American footballer Lionel Messi, despite the traditional football rivalry between Brazil and Argentina. In an interview with AS, Moura named Messi as his idol and praised his unique playing style, referring to him as the best player in the world. Ahead of their upcoming Champions League match against Barcelona, Moura acknowledges the challenge of facing Messi but is determined to work hard to stop him, expressing hope for a PSG victory. The teams will first clash at Parc des Princes, followed by a second match at the Nou Camp on April 21. PSG recently won the French League Cup.

A 49-year-old man, Eligah Christian, eluded Alaska State Troopers in a high-speed chase involving a motorhome, resulting in damaging several patrol cars. The chase took place outside Wasilla, a town known as former Vice President Sarah Palin's hometown. Christian was wanted on a $100,000 warrant for charges including scheming to defraud, theft, and issuing bad checks. Troopers eventually stopped Christian and charged him with felony reckless driving and criminal mischief, with him remaining in custody as of Sunday.

The glowing blue letters that once adorned Yankee Stadium in New York City, and were expected to sell for between $300,000 and $600,000 at a Sotheby's auction, failed to find a buyer. The letters, which were purchased by Yankee Hall of Famer Reggie Jackson after the stadium's final game in 2008, were installed in 1976 and had been a iconic part of the stadium's skyline. Jackson, who attended the auction, jokingly stated that he might have gotten ahead of himself when he purchased the letters on a whim. Former boxing trainer Bob Shannon, who helped Ricky Hatton prepare for his 2012 comeback fight against Vyacheslav Senchenko, has been charged with sexually assaulting a girl under the age of 16. Shannon, who also trained Hatton's brother Matthew to a European title and world title shot, is due before Manchester and Salford Magistrates on April 22, 2015. This charge comes after a report of a sexual assault that occurred in June 2014. Ricky Hatton, a former two-weight world champion, lost his comeback fight against Senchenko, which prompted his retirement from boxing.

Tottenham Hotspur are in discussions to extend midfielder Nabil Bentaleb's contract due to his impressive performances and potential interest from other clubs. The club aims to finalize the new deal promptly to prevent other teams from making offers. Bentaleb's new contract is expected to significantly increase his weekly wage to approximately £35,000, reflecting his importance to manager Mauricio Pochettino and his progress over the past 18 months.

Kenny Dalglish advises Harry Kane to skip the Under-21 European Championship this summer, suggesting that he should focus on preparing for Roy Hodgson's full England team for the 2016 European Championships. Dalglish believes Kane has shown he can compete with top-tier defenders and is ready to be a permanent member of the senior squad. He argues that the Under-21 team's role should be to develop players for the main squad, and Kane should not be bounced between the two teams to avoid fatigue. Spurs will be hoping to preserve their star striker's energy for the club season.

In a sold-out Providence Park match, Orlando City secured a 2-0 victory over Portland Timbers. Canadian international Cyle Larin scored the opening goal in the first half, swiftly reacting to a cross from Kevin Molino, and former Real Madrid star Kaka sealed the win with a penalty in the closing stages. Portland's Darlington Nagbe was the team's top threat, but a strong defensive performance from Orlando, led by Aurelien Collin, denied the Timbers opportunities and secured Orlando their second away win of the season.

A LaGuardia Airport worker in New York was filmed by NBC journalist Jeff Rossen doing push-ups on the runway after completing his work of loading a plane. The fitness enthusiast, wearing his high-visibility jacket and gloves, showed no sign of slowing down even after his 12th push-up. The video, captured on April 19, received numerous likes on Facebook, with comments praising the worker's dedication and strength. Despite the unusual sight, an airline worker nearby appeared unfazed by the scene.

Dirk Kuyt, a former Holland forward and former Feyenoord player, is returning to Feyenoord next season after a successful career with Liverpool, Fenerbahce, and the Netherlands. He signed a one-year contract with the club, currently third in the Eredivisie, and plans to extend his stay longer. Kuyt was a fan favorite during his first stint with Feyenoord from 2003-2006, when he was the Eredivisie's top scorer and was named Dutch footballer of the year. Feyenoord director Martin van Geel stated that they intend Kuyt's stay in Rotterdam to be longer, likening him to Ryan Giggs of

A truck crash on an elevated highway in Boston's Dorchester neighborhood caused delays for hundreds of morning commuters. The incident occurred on Interstate 93 around 6am, involving a box truck and a car. Both drivers were taken to the hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. As a precaution, officials ran replacement shuttle buses for southbound passengers on the subway and rail tracks, causing long queues. The truck driver, a 57-year-old man from Woburn, and the car driver, a 42-year-old man from Attleboro, were identified, but their names were not released. State troopers are still investigating the incident with no charges filed as of the article

Liverpool goalkeeper Simon Mignolet, despite not playing in Belgium's Euro 2016 qualifiers against Cyprus and Israel due to Thibaut Courtois' preference, returned to England and shared pictures of his aeroplane cockpit experience on his Facebook account. Mignolet is now back in time for Liverpool's crucial Premier League game against Arsenal on Saturday. The 27-year-old has regained his form after an initial dip in performance, becoming a key player in Liverpool's resurgence this season. He attributes his mental strength as a factor that helped him cope with the rough patch and continues to focus on improvement. Notably, Mignolet has kept six consecutive clean sheets in away Premier League games.

In their first meeting since taking control of Rangers, interim chairman Paul Murray and director John Gilligan discussed with Mike Ashley, owner of Newcastle United and Sports Direct, potential ways forward regarding the club's ownership. Ashley, who already holds an 8.92% stake in Rangers, has previously secured a loan of £5 million and the right to appoint two directors to the board, as well as a 75% grip on the Rangers Retail subsidiary. The new Ibrox board, which ousted Ashley's allies from the board, has declined to draw down the second £5 million loan from Sports Direct and instead opted for a £1.5 million loan from the Three Bears consortium. Liverpool striker Daniel Sturridge had a humorous encounter with comedian Darren Farley, who impersonated club legend Michael Owen, during a promotional video for BT Sport ahead of the FA Cup semi-final between Liverpool and Aston Villa. Sturridge found Farley's impression hilarious, struggling to contain his laughter. The conversation included discussions about the upcoming match and Sturridge's trademark goal celebration dance. Despite Farley's impressive Owen impression, his dancing was less successful. Sturridge is a doubt for the game due to a hip injury, while Villa have concerns over Gabriel Agbonlahor's hamstring strain and the suspension of Carlos Sanchez. The other FA Cup semi-final is scheduled between Arsenal and Reading.

The 9th US Circuit Court of Appeals has upheld the death sentence of Clark Elmore, a Washington man convicted of raping and killing his 14-year-old stepdaughter, Kristy Ohnstad, in 1995. The court found that the Washington Supreme Court did not act unreasonably in rejecting Elmore's claims of violated constitutional rights during his trial. Elmore, who is currently the second-longest serving inmate on Washington's death row, had claimed that his rights were violated due to bad legal advice and being shackled during the trial. The ruling does not automatically mean Elmore will be executed as Governor Jay Inslee has declared a moratorium on the death penalty. Elmore may

Swansea City is actively seeking to strengthen their squad for the upcoming season, particularly in the striker position. Manager Garry Monk has expressed interest in Blackburn Rovers' Rudy Gestede, who is valued at £7million, and Anderlecht's Aleksandar Mitrovic, as well as Club Brugge's Obbi Oulare. Swansea has also made an offer to Marseille's Andre Ayew, but he is a target for larger clubs as well. Financial Fairplay restrictions pose a challenge for Blackburn, as they need to sell players to balance their books, with Jordan Rhodes being a potential target for Middlesbrough, Derby, and Nottingham Forest. Approximately $70,000 worth of bull semen was stolen from a farm in southern Minnesota between April 1 and April 7. The stolen semen, housed in a $500 canister, was kept in an unlocked barn owned by farmer Daniel Weness. The market for bull semen exists due to its ability to help farmers save on transportation costs, allowing them to artificially inseminate their cows instead. The value of the semen can vary greatly, with higher prices associated with genetically superior bulls. Police have no suspects in the case at this time.

A popular radio host in Berkeley, California, Wesley Burton, was killed in a hit-and-run incident early Saturday morning while driving home from work. The crash occurred near the Berkeley-Oakland city line, and the hit-and-run driver fled the scene on foot. Burton, a father of three, worked at KPFA for 20 years and is survived by his wife Lucrecia and their children aged between 4 and 9. His wife has made an emotional plea for anyone with information about the accident to come forward. The family is seeking help with funeral costs and other expenses through a GoFundMe account. Police are investigating and have set up a hotline for anyone with information, with a $10,00

19-year-old German midfielder Serge Gnabry, who made a significant impact for Arsenal last season, is making a gradual return to the field after overcoming a year-long serious knee injury. He played a full 90 minutes for Arsenal's Under-21 team against Reading on Monday, marking his latest step towards recovery. Gnabry expressed optimism about his health, stating he is feeling increasingly better and is eager for his next game.

Bayern Munich's Bastian Schweinsteiger is doubtful for the German Cup quarter-final against Bayer Leverkusen on Wednesday due to an ankle injury sustained in their 1-0 victory over Borussia Dortmund. The Germany captain missed the opening four months of the season with a knee injury and Bayern's injury list has grown longer with reserve goalkeeper Tom Starke ruled out for four weeks. Other absent players include Arjen Robben, David Alaba, Holger Badstuber, and Franck Ribery, who is also nursing an ankle injury. Bayern, currently top of the league and progressing in the Champions League, will be hoping for a full recovery for these key players.

Manchester City football players, including Vincent Kompany and Gael Clichy, participated in a street party to unveil a 64-meter mural at the Academy Stadium. The mural, inspired by young City fans worldwide, reflects the positive influence of football on their lives and features images of football being played globally. Captain Kompany felt privileged to be a part of the event, noting the strong community connection of the City Football Academy. The club also recently invested £400,000 into a charitable program, Cityzens Giving, to extend the "football effect" to areas of need globally. However, the team's performance on the field has faltered, with a 2-1 defeat by Crystal Palace

Two 12-13-year-old boys are being hunted by police after they doused a 12-year-old schoolboy with WD40, a highly flammable substance, in an underpass in Newbury, Berkshire, and threatened to set him alight. The victim sustained minor injuries but did not require hospital treatment. The attack was described as unprovoked, and police are urging witnesses to come forward with information. The first offender is described as short, slim, with brown hair, and the second as stocky, about 5ft 4ins, with dark brown hair. Anyone with information is asked to contact the police.

Arsenal are nearing a £4.5million deal to sign 16-year-old Maxi Romero, often referred to as the next Lionel Messi, from Argentine club Velez Sarsfield. The Gunners are in advanced talks for the teenage sensation, with Raul Gamez, Velez president, confirming a substantial offer has been made. It is expected that Romero will remain at Velez on loan for at least two more seasons before joining Arsenal. He has been training with the first team at Velez but has yet to make his senior debut. Arsenal have a history of acquiring young talent, having recently signed Krystian Bielik in January.

Dani Alves' agent, Dinorah Santana, has confirmed that Barcelona has extended a new contract offer to the 31-year-old full-back, whose current contract expires in the summer. Despite interest from several top European clubs, including Manchester United and Liverpool, Alves is being courted by Barcelona, who are hoping to retain his services beyond this season. Santana has not yet revealed whether she is satisfied with the offer, but further discussions are expected to take place.

A pair of school bus drivers, Steven and Sarah Nick, are facing multiple felony charges for allegedly amassing a large stockpile of high-power weapons, including a 50-caliber machine gun, a sniper rifle, and 17,000 rounds of ammunition, in a secret basement bunker. The weapons were reportedly purchased with funds obtained illegally from the pair's mother-in-law's savings. Additionally, officers discovered bulletproof vests, including one for their nine-year-old daughter with the phrase "We kill for peace" on it. A hot air balloon collided with a transmission tower during a training session in Russia's Dmitrov district, resulting in a crash that left three occupants with cuts and bruises but in stable condition. The incident was captured on camera by a filmmaker who followed the balloon until it started plummeting, disintegrating, and crashing to the ground. After the crash, several people rushed to help the passengers as debris from the destroyed balloon rained down.

Mixed martial artist Conor McGregor has asserted that he could defeat boxing legend Floyd Mayweather in under 30 seconds if they ever competed against each other. McGregor, who is set to fight UFC featherweight champion Jose Aldo in July, argues that Mayweather's expertise in boxing alone would not be enough to counter McGregor's skills in other martial arts disciplines. This statement comes as Mayweather prepares for his highly-anticipated rematch with Manny Pacquiao in May, while McGregor continues his training for his UFC title fight. Dr. Alex Zhavoronkov, an anti-aging expert and director of the UK-based Biogerontology Research Foundation, believes advancements in medical technology and lifestyle choices will significantly extend human lifespan beyond current expectations. To achieve this, he adheres to a strict regime of regular exercise, drugs, supplements, and health checks, while foregoing traditional life milestones like marriage, children, and material assets in favor of focusing on anti-aging research. He is confident that this lifestyle will enable him to live up to 150 years. Manchester City is resisting Valencia's potential attempt to back out of a £24 million deal for Alvaro Negredo, citing a compulsory purchase clause in the loan agreement. Negredo's future at Valencia is in doubt following his lack of celebration after scoring against Levante, with speculation suggesting he could fund a move for Radamel Falcao. Valencia, however, faces financial difficulties and is reportedly looking to offload Negredo to afford Falcao, who is set to leave Manchester United at the end of his loan period. Meanwhile, Manchester City scouts have been watching FC Porto's Alex Sandro, a Brazilian left-back, as a potential new signing.

Tennis player Aljaz Bedene, who recently secured a British passport but is currently ineligible to represent Great Britain in the Davis Cup due to previous representations for Slovenia, has the support of former British player Greg Rusedski in his bid to overturn an ITF ruling. Rusedski, who himself switched from representing Canada to Great Britain, believes Bedene's inclusion in the top 100 would be beneficial for British tennis and could motivate other players. Andy Murray, the British No. 1, also supports Bedene's switch of nationality and believes it could serve as motivation for players below him. A Perth man, Milinda Gunasekera, pleaded guilty to groping a female passenger on a Qantas flight from Auckland Airport to Perth, following a holiday in Chile. The incident occurred while the plane was taxiing to the runway. Gunasekera, who admitted to being an alcoholic, downed a bottle of vodka in the airport toilets before the flight, leading to the incident. He has since sought help at a residential rehabilitation program and has not consumed alcohol since. He will return to New Zealand in June for sentencing.

Wallace, a 20-year-old defender on loan at Vitesse Arnhem from Chelsea, has been released without charge after being questioned by police on suspicion of a sexual offense. The incident occurred in Arnhem, and Vitesse immediately excluded Wallace from their squad for the match against Excelsior, fining him a maximum amount. The exact nature of the offense is still unclear, as an investigation is ongoing, but local media reports suggest it was sexual in nature. Wallace has previously been loaned out to Fluminense and Inter Milan since signing for Chelsea in January 2013.

Robin van Persie, the 32-year-old striker for Manchester United, is attracting interest from Italian clubs Juventus and Inter Milan this summer. He has been out of action for six weeks due to an ankle ligament injury, scoring 10 goals in the season so far. United, who are looking to strengthen their strikeforce, are considering offers for van Persie, particularly as Radamel Falcao is expected to return to Monaco. Another potential signing for United is Edinson Cavani of Paris Saint-Germain, a player who was also a target for former United boss David Moyes. A nanny, Lidia Quilligana, is in police custody after a video surfaced allegedly showing her physically abusing a 3-year-old girl in her care, including choking, beating, and burning her on a hot stove. The family had set up a nanny cam due to suspicions, and the video led to Quilligana's arrest in Danbury, Connecticut last month. However, her defense attorney claims the charges are false and that Quilligana was aware she was being filmed by the family. Four other families have written letters in support of Quilligana, stating she has been a trustworthy caregiver for their children in the past. She is currently out on $1 million bail and is scheduled to appear

FLIR Systems Inc., an Oregon-based defense contractor specializing in thermal imaging, has agreed to pay a $9.5 million settlement to resolve bribery charges brought by the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The charges involved expensive trips and gifts given to Middle Eastern government officials, resulting in over $7 million in profits for FLIR. Two employees in FLIR's Dubai office were found to have given luxury watches to five Saudi Arabia Ministry of Interior officials and arranged travel for Saudi officials, including an international trip lasting 20 nights. The SEC investigation revealed that the company's financial controls were deficient, failing to prevent the employees' activities. FLIR has since reported the misconduct and cooperated with the investigation. The former sales employees, Stephen

Carlos Tevez, a Juventus striker, has denied rumors about his potential return to Boca Juniors this summer, stating that he has not made a final decision on his future. While Tevez has performed the "chicken dance" celebration, a signature move from his Boca Juniors days, he has clarified that this celebration was not meant to suggest a return to his boyhood club. Tevez emphasized that he is focused on Juventus and is currently undecided about his future plans.

Matthew Kenney, a 34-year-old Florida resident, ran naked through traffic in Fort Lauderdale, believing that imaginary killers wanted to murder him. He had smoked Flakka, a designer drug also known as '$5 insanity', before the incident. Flakka, a synthetic stimulant, can cause hallucinations, psychosis, or even superhuman strength. Its use has risen significantly in Florida since 2010, with over 670 occurrences in 2014. Kenney has prior arrests for disorderly conduct, making a riot, and possession of a controlled substance, and was hospitalized for a psychiatric evaluation after the incident.

An Australian woman, Roxy Walsh, found a gold ring with a family crest and an engraving from a heartfelt message while snorkeling in Bali. She posted a photo of the ring on Facebook, hoping to reunite it with its owner, who appears to be named Joe or Jenny. The post has already been shared over 23 thousand times, including by The Bali Bible, with the aim of finding the ring's original owner. The search for Joe and Jenny is currently ongoing.

Hugo Lloris, Tottenham Hotspur's goalkeeper, will miss the Premier League match against Burnley on Sunday due to a knee injury sustained two weeks ago against Leicester City. Michel Vorm will replace Lloris in goal. Striker Roberto Soldado is also a doubt for the game, with Spurs needing a win to stay competitive in the race for a top-four finish. Manager Mauricio Pochettino expressed hope that four Tottenham players who played for England on Tuesday against Italy will return with increased confidence.

A North Carolina man, Daniel Steele, has been charged with murder after fatally shooting his six-month pregnant girlfriend, Kimberly Dianne Richardson, in Raleigh. The shooting occurred on Saturday night in the 3600 block of Sumner Boulevard. Richardson, a jewelry consultant, was previously married to a soldier and was six months pregnant when she died. Her newborn daughter, who survived, is currently receiving care at WakeMed hospital.

Liam Stewart, the 20-year-old ice hockey player and son of rock star Rod Stewart and former model Rachel Hunter, has had his senior Great Britain debut in the World Championship in Holland next week ended by an injury sustained during a play-off series. The injury has led to Craig Peacock of Belfast Giants replacing him in the squad. Stewart plays for the Spokane Chiefs, and although his mother, Rachel Hunter, was present to watch him, he will now miss the tournament due to his shoulder injury. The head coach, Pete Russell, expressed disappointment for Stewart but is confident about his future chances. Craig Peacock, who was initially left out of the original squad, will now take Stewart's place, and Russell acknowledges his talent

A healthcare worker who recently returned from Liberia, where she worked at an Ebola treatment clinic, has been admitted to Canberra Hospital with symptoms potentially indicative of Ebola. Although it's unlikely she has the virus, as her symptoms are consistent with various other conditions, she's being treated in complete isolation under the hospital's Ebola protocol. Officials emphasize that the risk of Ebola to the public is minimal, as the disease is primarily transmitted through direct contact with bodily fluids.

Celtic have expressed their concern over a controversial refereeing decision in their Scottish Cup semi-final loss to Inverness, specifically a handball by Josh Meekings that denied Leigh Griffiths a goal. The incident occurred during the first half, and although Celtic took an early lead through Virgil van Dijk's free-kick, they were unable to maintain it due to the controversy and subsequent events. The game ended in a 3-2 defeat for Celtic after extra-time, denying them a potential domestic Treble this season. Despite their disappointment, Celtic issued a statement congratulating Inverness on reaching their first-ever Scottish Cup final and praised them as a fantastic club.

A New York diner, identified as Nicholas Dematteis, lost his temper after waiting over an hour for his omelette at Bocca East restaurant on Saturday afternoon. He reportedly demanded a free meal, which led to a verbal altercation with a manager, during which he allegedly used a homophobic slur. Dematteis then physically assaulted the manager, grabbing him by the neck, slamming him against a bar, and pushing him into an elderly woman on the street. The woman was knocked to the ground but got up before the police arrived. Dematteis, who has a previous arrest record, was arrested and charged with assault.

A Belgian restaurant, Rubens eaterie, has been criticized as having the "stingiest boss in Europe" after charging a mother 36p to warm her baby's feeding bottle. The incident sparked outrage on Twitter, with many calling the manager petty and mean. The mother, Gitte Denteneer, shared a photo of the bill, which included a 50 cent charge for bottle warming, along with her meal and desserts totaling around £20. The restaurant chain later stated that the service is usually free, but the charge would only be levied if explicitly told to the customer.

Manchester City and Chelsea are vying to sign West Ham United's Aaron Cresswell, a 25-year-old English left-back, this summer. Cresswell has performed impressively in his first season with the Hammers since transferring from Ipswich. Both clubs are looking to increase their number of English players due to Premier League regulations requiring eight homegrown players in a 25-man squad. In addition to Cresswell, Manchester City is also reportedly interested in Liverpool's Raheem Sterling and Tottenham's Danny Rose for the left-back role, while players like Jack Wilshere, Jordan Henderson, and Sterling are on City's list to meet the homegrown quota.

Manchester United have offered David de Gea a record-breaking £200,000-a-week contract to discourage him from joining Real Madrid, who are reportedly interested in the Spanish goalkeeper when his contract with United ends next summer. United have expressed concerns about losing de Gea, their most outstanding performer this season, who was nominated for the PFA Player of the Year award. To prepare for the possibility of De Gea departing as a free agent next year, United have already signed former Barcelona keeper Victor Valdes. However, negotiations have been slow, and Louis Van Gaal has stated that the player ultimately holds the power to decide his future.

English cricketer Ian Bell, who made his professional debut with Warwickshire in 1999 after progressing through the youth ranks, has extended his contract with the club until the end of the 2017 season. Known for his successful international career, having played 105 Test matches and 161 one-day internationals for England, Bell has won five trophies with Warwickshire, including two County Championships in 2004 and 2012. Expressing his delight at the extension, Bell stated his desire to achieve further success both domestically and internationally. Tyler MacNiven, winner of the ninth season of The Amazing Race, and his fiancée, Kelly Hennigan, creatively announced their upcoming wedding by producing an action-packed three-minute save-the-date video. The video, inspired by action films, features MacNiven and Hennigan battling friends disguised as villains, escaping from an explosion, and ultimately confirming that guests should not make plans for September 26, 2015. The video ends with the couple enjoying a boat ride under the Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco, dressed in a tuxedo and wedding dress. Hennigan's brother is WWE superstar John Morrison, who may have contributed to the production's action elements

AC/DC drummer Phil Rudd has pleaded guilty to charges of threatening to kill and drug possession in a New Zealand court. The 60-year-old Australian was arrested last November following a search of his home, where methamphetamine and cannabis were found. Rudd is accused of threatening to kill his personal assistant after the failure of his solo album and a subsequent falling out with employees. His lawyer has stated that the charges are equivalent to an "angry phone call" and seeks to have Rudd discharged without conviction. Rudd could face up to seven years in prison for the threatening to kill charge, and his sentencing hearing is scheduled for June 26. The charge of attempting to procure murder was dropped in November.

Rebecca Grant, a 40-year-old woman from Biddeford, Maine, was arrested and charged with violating her bail conditions and criminal mischief after an allegedly violent encounter with police. During her arrest, she attempted to head-butt a deputy, scratched the paint of the police car with her teeth, bit the upholstery, and threatened to kill the deputies. The damage caused to the police car was estimated to be around $500. She was remanded in custody at the York County Jail in Alfred on a $200 cash bail.

A newborn baby girl was found dead in a garbage can outside a sorority house at Muskingum University in Ohio, reportedly suffocated after being born alive. The coroner's preliminary ruling suggests the baby was nearly full-term. The mother, a student at the university, has not been identified. This is the second such incident at the school; in 2002, a student named Jennifer Bryant was arrested and served seven months in prison for similar circumstances. Ohio has a Safe Haven Law that allows newborns to be anonymously left at hospitals or fire stations without facing legal charges, as long as there's no sign of abuse or neglect. Retired Brazilian footballer Ronaldo, a two-time Ballon d'Or winner, expressed his desire to play alongside Cristiano Ronaldo in the modern era, despite retiring in 2011. He suggested a potential return to play for Fort Lauderdale Strikers, a North American League team he now co-owns, if they reach the playoffs, but acknowledged that his playing days are over. Ronaldo admires the current performances and goal-scoring abilities of Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi, the leading lights of world football.

Dan Price, the CEO of Gravity Payments, has decided to drastically reduce his own salary by 93% and institute a minimum wage of $70,000 for all employees, including junior staff. This move will account for 80% of the company's $2.2 million expected profit. Price, who founded Gravity Payments in 2004, was inspired to make this change after reading a study about the impact of pay raises on happiness for individuals earning less than $70,000 per year. This move will significantly affect his personal income, as he currently takes home a significant portion of the company's profits. Dikembe Mutombo, an eight-time NBA All-Star renowned for his shot-blocking abilities and iconic finger-wagging gesture, was inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame on Monday. Throughout his 18-year NBA career from 1991 to 2009, Mutombo tallied 11,729 points, 12,359 rebounds, and 3,289 blocks, primarily with Denver, Atlanta, and Houston. The article reports that Aaron Tate, 29, and Rachel Jaajaa, 22, have been charged with child endangerment after their three-month-old daughter was found alone at a McDonald's in Elyria, Ohio. The incident occurred over the weekend when the parents allegedly couldn't agree on who should take care of the baby. Tate is accused of leaving his daughter on the floor of the McDonald's and driving away, while Jaajaa refused to take the baby because it was the father's turn. The baby was discovered by a McDonald's patron who called 911, and police found the parents arguing in front of a Burger King later. Jaajaa's sister is now caring for the baby,

Derby County is considering Paul Clement as a replacement for current manager Steve McClaren, who is also a target for Newcastle United. Clement is currently a coach at Real Madrid, working under Carlo Ancelotti. If McClaren leaves for Newcastle, as expected, Derby plans to make formal contact with Real Madrid. Clement has established a reputation as a leading European coach and Derby is offering him the opportunity to cut his managerial teeth at Pride Park. However, McClaren could potentially decide to stay at Derby if they secure promotion to the Premier League next season, but Derby is preparing for life without him.

Didier Drogba, the Ivorian footballer, has announced he will continue playing next season, despite uncertainty over his contract with Chelsea, where he returned last summer on a 12-month deal. At 37, Drogba finds it frustrating to be a bit-part player due to the summer signings of Diego Costa and Loic Remy, but he's ready to play whenever needed. He's hopeful of winning his fourth Premier League title with Chelsea, who are currently top of the league. Drogba emphasized the importance of staying focused and giving their best in every game to maintain their league position.

A seven-year-old British schoolgirl, India Mayhew, died from severe head injuries after a horse she was riding bolted at a facility in Matheran, India. The incident occurred during a family holiday, with India's father, Gavin Mayhew, just meters away. The family, which included India's mother, brother, and two sisters, were also riding in the facility's grounds. The local police reported that Gavin saw the entire incident and stated it was an accident. The police registered a case of accidental death, and the family has chosen not to press charges against the facility operator. He justified the payments by claiming he provided 'kudos' for the school. The team is currently in a relegation battle, with only one win in nine games, including a 4-1 loss to Crystal Palace in their last match. Sebastien Pocognoli, a Belgian defender, has expressed confusion over losing his starting place in the West Brom team under manager Tony Pulis, despite being an important player under previous manager Alan Irvine. Pocognoli, who feels happy in Birmingham and has a good relationship with the fans, has admitted his frustration over not getting playing time, even when his left-back rival is injured or suspended. He has hinted at considering his long-term future, as he awaits the club's plans at the end of the season, but emphasizes he doesn't want to cause trouble. The season has been turbulent for West Brom, but has shown improvement since Pulis took over from Irvine.

In the 1-0 win against Burnley, Everton manager Roberto Martinez defended Ross Barkley's penalty miss after he opted to take the kick instead of regular penalty-taker Leighton Baines, who has scored 15 out of 16 attempts in the Premier League. Barkley's missed penalty brought back memories of a previous incident with Kevin Mirallas taking the penalty against West Brom and missing. Martinez defended Barkley's decision, stating that he has several players who can take penalties, and it depends on how they feel at the moment. A 60-year-old businessman named Phil Haste received a £50 fine for failing to display his tax disc, despite the requirement for it being abolished six months prior. The fine was issued by Torbay council, who also alleged that he had not shown a pay and display ticket in his car, despite having a valid car parking permit on his dashboard. Mr. Haste is refusing to pay the fine, stating it is "diabolical" and is a result of the council's lack of understanding regarding the new laws. He has since appealed the fine, and Torbay Council has responded by stating they do not issue fines for road tax offenses and have advised Mr. Haste on how to appeal the ticket.

Real Madrid goalkeeper Iker Casillas, facing constant criticism for his errors this season, kept a clean sheet during a 2-0 win against Rayo Vallecano. The Bernabeu crowd has been unforgiving towards Casillas, who has been booed by his own supporters. Casillas responded to questions about his future in the team, stating that decisions about the starting lineup are made by the coach, and the team's main focus is to win and hope Barcelona doesn't. Casillas was pleased with his performance in the game, as the team kept a clean sheet. Cristiano Ronaldo scored the 300th goal of his Real Madrid career during the match.

English cricket player Moeen Ali, a lifelong fan of Liverpool Football Club, has been appointed as an honorary ambassador for Liverpool's fan club in Pakistan, known as the Pak Reds. The 27-year-old, who has Pakistani descent, expressed his excitement about this new association and shared a photo with Liverpool players Raheem Sterling and Emre Can on his Twitter account. The Pak Reds, established in 2011 and granted official status in 2013, has branches across several cities in Pakistan. In the village of Alderholt, Dorset, an unidentified individual or group mysteriously delivered a chocolate egg to every one of the 3,000 households, leaving residents astonished and touched. The generous gesture was met with joy and excitement, with residents speculating about the origin of this unique act of kindness, drawing comparisons to the popular TV show "The Vicar of Dibley."

The BBC is set to air a two-hour real-time documentary, titled "All Aboard! The Canal Trip," capturing a leisurely canalboat journey at 4mph along the Kennet and Avon Canal. The footage, shot from a single camera on the front of the boat, features no narrator, dialogue, or music, allowing viewers to enjoy the natural sounds of the environment. The documentary aims to create a mesmeric, hypnotic, and beautiful sensory experience, with the producer likening it to a Constable landscape.
