Gareth Bale, despite suffering a calf injury that rules him out of Real Madrid's Champions League quarter-final second leg against Atletico, has shown support for his team by wishing them luck on Facebook. Bale's injury occurred during the 3-1 win against Malaga on the weekend. Real Madrid will also be without Karim Benzema and Luka Modric for the match due to injuries. Manager Carlo Ancelotti has expressed confidence in his options, potentially starting Isco, Jese, and Javier Hernandez in the absence of the key trio. Jese is expected to replace Bale in attack for Real against Atletico at the Santiago Bernabeu.

A wildfire, sparked by a cooking stove, forced over 300 residents in Southern California to evacuate their homes in the middle of the night. The fire, located along the border of Norco and Corona cities, 35 miles southeast of downtown Los Angeles, grew to 1.6 square miles by Sunday midday, with 35% contained. The area's dry vegetation and lack of water in the Prado Dam Flood Control Basin, where the fire was burning, posed challenges. Residents were initially urged to stay indoors due to the smoke but were allowed to return home before dawn on Sunday. Charlene Mess, a 48-year-old woman, has been arrested for allegedly murdering her husband Douglas Mess, aged 52, at their farm in Attica, New York. The body was found buried under a pile of manure on the 300-acre farm, and Charlene remained on the property during an extensive search that took seven hours. Authorities have charged Charlene with second-degree murder, and she is being held without bail by the Village of Warsaw Court. The couple had lived on the farm for the past four years, and Attica is a rural town of 7,000 residents located approximately 40 miles southeast of Buffalo, New York.

Jenson Button was penalized for crashing into Pastor Maldonado during the Chinese Grand Prix, which resulted in Maldonado's retirement and damage to Button's front wing. The incident occurred as they were racing for 13th place. Button received a five-second penalty post-race, dropping him from 13th to 14th, and was given two penalty points on his superlicence. He expressed regret, stating it was a misjudgment. The incident marked a disappointing weekend for McLaren, with Button and teammate Fernando Alonso struggling in the lower ranks of the field.

The lawyers for Michael Brown's parents, who were an 18-year-old African-American fatally shot by a white police officer in Ferguson, Missouri, announced plans to file a wrongful death lawsuit against the city the following day. This lawsuit, expected since early March, will also name former officer Darren Wilson, who shot Brown but resigned in November. The lawsuit comes after a Justice Department report in March highlighted racial bias and profiling within the Ferguson Police Department and a profit-driven municipal court system that disproportionately targeted black residents, leading to several resignations and a fired court clerk for racist emails. Defender Chris Smalling, a key member of Manchester United's defense under Louis van Gaal, recently joined Twitter and shared a photo from a barbecue with friends and girlfriend. Smalling's performance could be crucial in United's upcoming game against Manchester City, as they aim to extend their lead in the race for the second automatic Champions League qualification place. Meanwhile, teammate Adnan Januzaj, who has struggled for game time this season, also joined Twitter and expressed his desire for more playing time.

An Albuquerque man, identified as Sergio Barrientos-Hinojosa, is accused of using his 13-year-old son as a getaway driver after opening fire at a customer he argued with at a gas station. No one was injured. Police believe Barrientos-Hinojosa, who was reportedly intoxicated, had his son drive him to buy beer, where the argument ensued. He allegedly fired several shots, with the 13-year-old driving away. The incident resulted in charges of child abuse against Barrientos-Hinojosa, who was also found to have discarded his gun near a family's house with numerous children present. He is reportedly without an attorney at this time.

In the Indian city of Malegaon, police are requiring identity cards for cattle due to a state-wide ban on beef. Residents are asked to submit photographs of their cows, providing details about their unique features, to create a database. This is intended to help enforce the ban on cow slaughter, as cows are revered by the majority Hindu population. Over 100 owners have complied, while beef traders have protested and threatened legal action against the decision. Other Indian states, such as Haryana, have also implemented tougher penalties for cow slaughter, with the Home Minister aiming to devise a nationwide law against it.

In a U21 league match at Wheatsheaf Park, Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur played out a goalless draw. Both teams missed chances to win, with Spurs dominating the second half. Ruben Loftus-Cheek played for Chelsea, but was forced off injured early. DeAndre Yedlin impressed for Spurs in the right-back position. The draw kept Chelsea eighth in the table, while Spurs moved up to third, behind Manchester United and Liverpool. Despite the result, Spurs' coach Ugo Ehiogu was encouraged by his team's performance.

In Yichuan, Shaanxi province, over 1,700 Chinese high school students took their exams in the school's playground due to a lack of indoor space. The students, particularly the freshmen, came up with the idea and organized the outdoor exam themselves. To combat sunlight glare, the exam was interrupted periodically for students to rotate their desks. Many students found this unconventional method refreshing, broadening their horizons, and potentially helpful in preventing cheating. The school viewed this as a test of the students' organizational skills.

A Coast Guard cutter, the Boutwell, arrived in San Diego on Thursday with 14 tons of seized cocaine, valued at $424 million, following 19 separate incidents in the eastern Pacific Ocean near Central and South America. This recent seizure is part of a surge of drug interdictions in the region, with U.S., Canadian, and Canadian naval forces seizing over 28 tons of cocaine worth $848 million and making 101 arrests in the last six months. The Boutwell had previously offloaded over 14 tons of cocaine to the DEA in October, marking the second such large drug seizure for the ship in six months.

Jose Mourinho has declared Eden Hazard as one of the best three players in the world, praising his exceptional talent and humility. The Chelsea winger scored the winning goal against Manchester United, extending their lead at the top of the Premier League. Hazard believes he is having the best season of his career, citing improved management of strong and weak moments in games compared to last year. Mourinho expects Hazard to win the PFA Player of the Year award this season.
